The '''soul stealer''' was a talisman that attempted to possess the bodies of nearby individuals and compel them to try and [[Death/Legends|kill]] others. At some point, the [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]] became a part of the [[Collector/Legends|collection]] maintained by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Humat]]. He kept the soul stealer in the [[Cell of Imu]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], the Cell of Imu became buried under rising [[Skyscraper/Legends|skyscrapers]], but eventually a group of individuals managed to enter it and retrieve the soul stealer.

==Description==
The soul stealer was a [[Milk/Legends|milky]]-[[Color/Legends|white]] talisman that had the ability to attempt to possess the bodies of individuals present in the same room with it and compel those individuals to [[Death/Legends|kill]] anyone they saw.<ref name="Coruscant" />

==History==
At some point, the [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]] fell into the possession of [[Humat]], an eccentric [[Jedi/Legends|member]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] who lived more than nineteen [[Millennium/Legends|thousand years]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The soul stealer subsequently became the centerpiece of Humat's [[Collector/Legends|collection]] of unusual mystical artifacts, many of which had evil applications.<ref name="Coruscant" />

Humat stored his collection on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the catacomb known as the [[Cell of Imu]], which was protected from would-be [[Thief/Legends|thieves]] by deadly traps, with the soul stealer being kept in the innermost chamber of the structure. The Cell of Imu became buried over the following millennia under the rising [[Skyscraper/Legends|skyscrapers]] of the [[Petrax Historic Quarter|Petrax Quarter]].<ref name="Coruscant" />

{{RPGseed}}
The catacomb became uncovered at some point, however, when a [[Construction droid/Legends|construction droid]] razed one of the district's tall buildings, allowing [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologists]] to access the Cell of Imu. Subsequently, a group of individuals were recruited to penetrate the structure's traps. The members of the group managed to do so and retrieve the soul stealer despite the artifact attempting to possess them.<ref name="Coruscant" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The soul stealer was introduced in the [[2003]] sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', which was published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the artifact was featured in a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure, which this article assumes plays out as described.<ref name="Coruscant" />
